pull.title: wallet: use `outpoint` when estimating input size
pull.body:
### Problem
`CalculateMaximumSignedInputSize()` is passed the outpoint being sized, but a previous refactor stopped using that context when estimating the signed input size.
This could make externally selected inputs look slightly smaller than they really are.

### Fix
Pass the outpoint through again when estimating the signed input size.
Add a regression test for the external-input case.

> [!NOTE]
> the branch name still reflects the previous state of this PR, where the unused parameter was removed instead of wired back in

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35228. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Stale ACK | [w0xlt](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35228#pullrequestreview-4241856646), [stickies-v](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35228#pullrequestreview-4242150424), [pablomartin4btc](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35228#pullrequestreview-4242337305) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#34617](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34617) (fees: wallet: remove block policy fee estimator internals from wallet by ismaelsadeeq) * [#34075](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34075) (fees: Introduce Mempool Based Fee Estimation to reduce overestimation by ismaelsadeeq) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first. ### LLM Linter (✨ experimental) Possible places where named args for integral literals may be used (e.g. `func(x, /*named_arg=*/0)` in C++, and `func(x, named_arg=0)` in Python): - `MaxInputWeight(*desc, CTxIn{outpoint}, coin_control, true, can_grind_r)` in `src/wallet/spend.cpp` 2026-05-13 08:46:24

Actually I'm not sure that this is correct. I thought there was a test for the thing that the parameter would have controlled, but it seems like there maybe isn't. Will need to look at this further in more detail.

> I'm not sure that this is correct. 

The original take was to use the dead method parameter instead, see: https://github.com/l0rinc/bitcoin/pull/161/changes#diff-6e06b309cd494ef5da4e78aa0929a980767edd12342137f268b9219167064d13R97
I can see the param use within the function was removed in commit 9b7ec39 at #26567, perhaps the function signature should have been updated there?
Maybe @darosior knows more about the expected behavior

wallet: use outpoint when estimating input size

`CalculateMaximumSignedInputSize()` is passed the outpoint being sized, but that context was not used when estimating the signed input size.
Pass the outpoint through so externally selected inputs are not underestimated.

Co-authored-by: Antoine Poinsot <darosior@protonmail.com>

events.body:
Thanks a lot for checking, @darosior.
I have reverted the code to the [original implementation](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35228#issuecomment-4395930399), which rewired the parameter.
I adjusted the PR description and commit message, and added you as co-author.
